sources_exist.note: All seven canonical citations resolved to accessible content, and the four deterministic dataset inputs exactly match the supplied derived dataset.

figures_match_sources.note: The linked consulting-multiple page supports the buy and exit ranges. The adoption, historical-analog, owner-age, consolidator-count, and pricing-mix claims are not established by their cited pages.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The role fractions are bounded but are conditioned on energy consulting being dominant, which the cited IBISWorld page does not establish within NAICS 541690. pi_dist=0.45 relies on unsupported billing weights, and t50=7 relies on an unsupported adoption baseline and historical diffusion rate.

rubric_consistent.note: The record treats unsupported figures as MED research inputs instead of ESTIMATE chains, affecting A, B, and C. ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on S is within the orchestrator's tolerance and is not independently disqualifying.

cross_checks_honest.note: The uplift, terminal-value, and no-proxy narratives are candid conditional analyses, but the pricing-deflation cross-check repeats an unevidenced 30%/35%/20%/15% billing mix and the adoption cross-check relies on an unsupported technology timeline.

source_audits.note: PrivSource is a broad consulting-acquisition list and supports some relevant deals, including E Source/ILLUME in utilities and Aperture/QSI in forensic economics. It does not substantiate the exact six-platform list in the record or show that all six made in-scope 541690 add-ons during the last 24 months.

The page reports 7-9x EBITDA for $1M-$3M specialty consulting and 8-11x for $3M-$5M specialty consulting. The selected 8.0x is a disclosed midpoint judgment within those cited ranges, though not a transaction median specific to 541690.

Census reports 17-20% overall business AI use and less than 20% for firms with four or fewer employees. This page does not report the quoted 33% professional-services rate or support the upward adjustment to 22% for energy consulting.

The page reports 9-13x EBITDA for $5M-$10M specialty consulting and 11-15x for $10M-$25M specialty consulting. A 10.0x exit assumption lies within the former range, though the linked page does not establish the additional Guidehouse or energy-advisory transaction claims.

The podcast page discusses AI adoption and innovation at one large AEC firm, but contains no EnergyPlus/eQUEST history, no early-2000s to 2010/2012 timeline, and no 8-12-year early-adopter-to-majority benchmark.

The First Citizens page is an energy M&A outlook focused on upstream, midstream, power, and infrastructure dealmaking. It contains no small-business owner-age statistic, no 37% Boomer-owner figure, and no succession evidence.

The accessible IBISWorld page reports $7.3 billion revenue and 4,499 businesses and defines a broad custom industry that includes management, environmental, and technology consulting. It does not report the quoted $7.1 billion/4,419 figures or the asserted 30% shared-savings, 35% project-fee, 20% subscription, and 15% cost-plus revenue mix.

reasons: inputs.current_adoption_pct at https://www.census.gov/library/stories/2026/05/ai-use-businesses.html uses a 0.22 energy-consulting estimate and quotes a 33% professional-services rate absent from the linked page; rerun with a direct sector benchmark or label the adjustment ESTIMATE and provide a complete sourced derivation.

inputs.historical_analogs at https://engineeringmanagementinstitute.org/aect-070-exploring-era-tech-adoption-and-innovation-aec/ does not establish 8-10 years to widespread EnergyPlus/eQUEST use, so inputs.t50_years and score A lack an auditable analog; rerun with dated adoption evidence identifying the start and majority-use points.

inputs.owners_60plus_pct at https://newsroom.firstcitizens.com/M-A-Sector-Spotlight-Energy-2025-Outlook is wholly unrelated to owner demographics and succession, yet its 0.37 value and succession bonus affect B; rerun with a comparable owner-age source and a directly supported succession finding.

inputs.active_consolidators at https://www.privsource.com/acquisitions/private-equity-backed-acquisitions/consulting does not validate the exact six-platform count for in-scope 541690 add-ons in the last 24 months; rerun with named sponsors, targets, dates, and NAICS-scope reasoning for every counted platform.

inputs.pricing_structure at https://www.ibisworld.com/united-states/industry/energy-utility-consulting-services/6132/ provides no support for the 30%/35%/20%/15% billing mix that drives inputs.pi_dist and cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation; rerun with direct billing-share evidence or a clearly labeled ESTIMATE chain, and establish that energy consulting is actually the dominant 541690 subsegment before applying its role and capture model.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on S
